title: ExecutionSurface
description: The sandboxing and runtime constraints of an agent's execution environment: PTY, working directory, sandbox mode, network mode, and protected path list.

pty.description: Whether a pseudo-terminal (PTY) should be allocated for the agent process.

workdir.description: Absolute path to the working directory for the agent session.

background.description: Whether the agent runs as a background (non-interactive) process.

reviewOnly.description: When true, the agent may read and analyse but must not modify anything.

worktreeStrategy.description: How git worktrees are managed: none (no worktree), existing (use current), create-temp (ephemeral), or named (persistent named worktree).

sandboxMode.description: Isolation level: none, user-level sandbox, container, VM, or browser sandbox.

networkMode.description: Network access: none (air-gapped), allowlist (egress restricted to egressAllowlist), or full (unrestricted).

egressAllowlist.description: Hostnames or CIDR ranges permitted for outbound network access when networkMode is "allowlist".

elevated.description: Whether the agent runs with elevated (e.g. root or administrator) privileges.

protectedPaths.description: File system paths the agent must not read from or write to.

approvalProfile.description: Optional identifier of a human-approval profile that governs when the agent must pause and wait for explicit sign-off.
